title: Efficient clear ice cube production
abstract: An apparatus for making ice cubes that comprises at least one elongated mould (102) that defines a space (117) for an ice column, which space (117) is at least substantially closed at least while a liquid substance is being refrigerated. A supplying device (118) is provided for supplying the liquid substance to the at least one elongated mould (102). A refrigerating device (111) is provided for freezing the liquid substance inside the at least one elongated mould (102) An elongated element (101) is configured to extend through said at least one mould (102) in a longitudinal direction of said at least one mould (102). The elongated element (101) is configured to rotate around a longitudinal axis of the elongated element (101).
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1 . An apparatus for making ice cubes, comprising at least one elongated mould (102) that defines a space (1 17) for an ice column, which space (1 17) is at least substantially closed at least while a liquid substance is being refrigerated; a supplying device (1 18) for supplying the liquid substance to the at least one elongated mould (102); a refrigerating device (1 1 1 ) for freezing the liquid substance inside the at least one elongated mould (102); and an elongated element (101 ) configured to extend through said at least one mould (102) in a longitudinal direction of said at least one mould (102); wherein the elongated element (101 ) is configured to rotate around a longitudinal axis of the elongated element (101 ), and wherein an inside surface (107) of the mould (102) comprises a recess (1 13) at an end of the mould (102) in the longitudinal direction of the mould (102), preferably at a bottom of the mould (102), wherein the recess (1 13) is configured to receive a tip (1 15) of the elongated element. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein at least part of an outside surface of the elongated element (101 ) is coarse, bristly, or uneven. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 or 2, wherein the outside surface of the elongated element (101 ) has at least one protrusion and/or at least one recess and/or is non- cylindrical in cross section. 4. The apparatus of any preceding claim, comprising an actuator (1 10) operatively coupled to the elongated element (101 ) to control the rotation of the elongated element (101 ). 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the actuator (1 10) is configured to cause the elongated element (101 ) to rotate at least during a part of a time during which the liquid substance is being refrigerated. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, further comprising a temperature sensor to detect a temperature of the mould, wherein the actuator is configured to cause the elongated element to rotate at a first rotation speed when the detected temperature is above zero degrees Celsius, and at a second rotation speed when the detected temperature is below zero degrees Celsius, wherein the second rotation speed is higher than the first rotation speed. 7. The apparatus of claim 5 or 6, wherein the actuator is configured to continue causing the elongated element to rotate after the freezing is completed. 8. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein a wall of the mould (102) at an end of the mould (108) in the longitudinal direction, preferably at a top of the mould, comprises an orifice (109) through which the elongated element (101 ) is configured to extend. 9. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the orifice (109) in the wall of the mould (102) is at least partly covered with a flexible solid material (1 12). 10. The apparatus of claim 8 or 9, wherein the actuator (1 10) is mechanically coupled to a portion (1 16) of the elongated element (101 ), which portion (1 16) extends from the mould (102). 1 1 . The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein at least one wall (1 19) of the mould (102) comprises at least part of the refrigerating device (1 1 1 ). 12. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein at least part of an inside surface (105) of the mould comprises a metal, such as aluminium. 13. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein said at least one mould (102) defines a series of interconnected, hollow spaces for forming an elongated ice column of interconnected ice cubes, or wherein the apparatus comprises a plurality of moulds (6) which are oriented in a matrix relative to each other. 14. A method of making ice cubes, comprising supplying (201 ) a liquid substance to at least one elongated mould that defines a space for an ice column, which space is at least substantially closed at least while the liquid substance is being refrigerated; freezing (202) the liquid substance inside the at least one elongated mould, while rotating an elongated element extending through the mould in a longitudinal direction of the mould around a longitudinal axis of the elongated element, wherein an inside surface of the mould comprises a recess at an end of the mould in the longitudinal direction of the mould, preferably at a bottom of the mould, wherein the recess receives a tip of the elongated element; and removing (203) the ice column thus formed from the mould.
